prompt: I want to move the scanner effect processing to WASM. The applyScannerEffect function in image-effects.ts does grayscale conversion, brightness, contrast, yellowish tint, and random noise — all per-pixel in a JS loop. The blur and border/rotation parts use Canvas APIs so those should stay in JS. Just the pixel processing kernel needs to go to Rust.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies the WASM/JS boundary — pixel processing in Rust, Canvas blur/border/rotation stays in JS
Rust function handles grayscale, brightness, contrast, yellowish tint, and noise
Replaces Math.random noise with a seedable PRNG (deterministic for testing)
TypeScript integration passes pixel buffer to WASM and applies Canvas operations after
Does NOT move Canvas-dependent code (blur, border gradient, rotation) into Rust
